description: Prefer function components with hooks over class components.
suggestion: Convert to a function component and use hooks (useState/useEffect/useRef).

examples.bad: class MyComp extends React.Component { render(){ return <div/> } }

description: Prefer HeroUI primitives (Button, Input, Avatar, DropdownItem, etc.) when a matching component exists instead of raw HTML so styling and accessibility are consistent.
suggestion: Use the corresponding HeroUI component (e.g. <Button>, <Input>, <Avatar />) unless you need a raw element for a compelling reason.

description: DropdownItem with non-plain text children must expose a machine-readable textValue prop for accessibility.
suggestion: Add a textValue prop with a short text summary (not passed to DOM directly). Example: <DropdownItem textValue="My Profile">...jsx...</DropdownItem>

description: Uploads to storage should use owner-scoped paths (avatars/{uid}/...) and not public/global locations.
suggestion: Ensure uploads use `avatars/${uid}/filename` and storage rules enforce request.auth.uid == userId.

description: Do not commit API keys, service account JSON, or other secrets to the repository.
suggestion: Move secrets into environment variables or a secret manager and update code to read from process.env or runtime config.

description: Effects that subscribe to external resources must return a cleanup function to avoid leaks.
suggestion: Return a cleanup function from useEffect. For example: useEffect(()=>{ const sub = x.subscribe(); return ()=>sub.unsubscribe() }, []);

description: Props and public hooks should be strongly typed in TypeScript (no implicit any).
suggestion: Add explicit types or generics to props and hook return values. Enable strict mode in tsconfig.

examples.good: type Props = { title: string }; function Comp(props:Props){ return null }

description: Interactive elements must be semantic and keyboard accessible; e.g., use button for actions or add role/tabindex with correct handlers.
suggestion: Use <button> for clickable actions or ensure correct ARIA roles and keyboard handlers are present.

description: For server state caching prefer React Query patterns (useQuery/useMutation) instead of ad-hoc fetch + local state for non-trivial data fetching.
suggestion: Wrap server calls in hooks and surface them via useQuery/useMutation to benefit from caching/retries/optimistic updates.
